name: DST spring-forward boundary: late-night captures resolve to the intended LOCAL day, distinct instants
description: US Mountain DST begins 2026-03-08 02:00 (MST -07:00 -> MDT -06:00). Note A is captured the night BEFORE the change (2026-03-07T23:30:00-07:00) logging a glucose reading 'tonight' -> local day March 7, offset -07:00. Note B is captured the night AFTER (2026-03-08T23:30:00-06:00) logging 'tonight' -> local day March 8, offset -06:00. Authoring the model's job (harness rule: resolved temporals are hand-authored), each valid_from is the intended LOCAL instant with the correct same-day offset, so the two readings are DISTINCT instants. Because these are measurements at different instants, decide() accumulates both (no same-instant clash, no fact_conflict). Pins that a late-evening capture near a DST boundary does NOT get its valid_from skewed onto the wrong UTC calendar day and that the offset flips with the rule (the two reported_at instants and the two valid_from instants both straddle the transition correctly).

facts.statement: Glucose was 95 mg/dL on the night of 2026-03-07.
Glucose was 102 mg/dL on the night of 2026-03-08.
